title: There shall be at most one occurrence of the # or ## operators in a single macro definition
description: There shall be at most one occurrence of the # or ## operators in a single macro definitionAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellpreprocessormisra-c++2008misra-c2004misra-c2012Why is this an issue?More InfoBecause the evaluation order of#and##are not specified, the results of using them both in the same macro could be unpredictable. Therefore macros should contain at most once instance of either#or##.Noncompliant code example#define NonCompliant(a, b) # a ## b int main() { std::cout << NonCompliant(Hello, World); }The result of this code is unspecified. It will either print "HelloWorld" or trigger a compilation error.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
#define PROBLEM(x, y) #x ## y ## _suffix  // Noncompliant: # and multiple ##
#define ANOTHER(a, b, c) #a ## b ## #c       // Noncompliant: multiple # and ##
compliant:
#define StringOnly(a) # a              // Compliant: only # operator
#define ConcatOnly(a, b) a ## b         // Compliant: only ## operator

#define STRINGIFY(x) #x                // Compliant: single # operator
#define CONCAT(a, b) a ## b            // Compliant: single ## operator
#define MAKE_NAME(prefix, suffix) prefix ## suffix  // Compliant

// Separate macros instead of combining operators
#define STR(x) #x
#define CAT(a, b) a ## b
#define MAKE_STRING_VAR(name) STR(name) "_var"  // Compliant: separate usage

checks.message: There shall be at most one occurrence of the # or ## operators in a single macro definition
